title: MBLEx D1 Anatomy & Physiology v2 (aligned choices)

questions.prompt: Which structure carries urine from the kidneys to the bladder?
questions.why: Ureters transport urine from kidneys to bladder; the urethra carries urine out of the body.
questions.trap: Urethra is the exit tube, not the kidney-to-bladder tube.

questions.prompt: Which blood vessel type has the thickest muscular walls?
questions.why: Arteries withstand higher pressure and have thicker smooth muscle layers.
questions.trap: Veins are lower-pressure return vessels.

questions.prompt: Which of the following structure in the lung conducts air but is not a primary gas-exchange surface?
questions.why: Bronchi are conducting airways; gas exchange occurs at alveoli.
questions.trap: Capillaries exchange gases with alveoli but are not airways.

questions.prompt: Erythropoietin (EPO) primarily:
questions.why: EPO from the kidneys signals bone marrow to produce more red blood cells.
questions.trap: Blood glucose is regulated by insulin/glucagon.

questions.prompt: Which structure is commonly called the windpipe?
questions.why: The trachea conducts air from larynx toward bronchi.
questions.trap: Esophagus carries food, not air.

questions.prompt: The adrenal medulla primarily secretes:
questions.why: The adrenal medulla releases catecholamines for acute stress responses.
questions.trap: Cortisol and aldosterone are adrenal cortex hormones.

questions.prompt: Which skin layer contains most blood vessels and many sensory receptors?
questions.why: The dermis contains vessels, nerves, and connective tissue supporting skin function.
questions.trap: The epidermis is avascular and relies on diffusion from the dermis.

questions.prompt: A main function of skin is to:
questions.why: Skin protects against pathogens, injury, and excessive water loss.
questions.trap: Urine filtration is kidney function.

questions.prompt: The sympathetic nervous system is most associated with:
questions.why: Sympathetic activation prepares the body for acute stress responses.
questions.trap: Rest-and-digest is parasympathetic.

questions.prompt: Which valve is located between the right atrium and right ventricle?
questions.why: The tricuspid valve is the right AV valve.
questions.trap: Mitral is the left AV valve.

questions.prompt: Which structure is most responsible for maintaining airway patency during swallowing?
questions.why: The epiglottis covers the airway opening during swallowing to prevent aspiration.
questions.trap: Tracheal rings keep airway open, but epiglottis specifically protects during swallowing.

questions.prompt: What is the primary role of the large intestine?
questions.why: The large intestine reabsorbs water and compacts waste into stool.
questions.trap: Bile is produced by liver; insulin by pancreas.

questions.prompt: What is the primary role of red blood cells?
questions.why: Hemoglobin in red blood cells carries oxygen to tissues.
questions.trap: Clotting is primarily a platelet function.

questions.prompt: Which cell type is most directly involved in transporting oxygen in blood?
questions.why: Red blood cells contain hemoglobin for oxygen transport.
questions.trap: Platelets help with clotting, not oxygen transport.

questions.prompt: What is the primary role of thyroid hormones (T3/T4)?
questions.why: Thyroid hormones broadly influence metabolic activity and energy use in tissues.
questions.trap: Blood sugar is regulated by insulin/glucagon, not thyroid hormones.

questions.prompt: Melanin primarily helps:
questions.why: Melanin absorbs UV radiation and helps protect against sun damage.
questions.trap: Glucose regulation is insulin/glucagon, not melanin.

questions.prompt: Which structure is the main site of urine storage?
questions.why: The urinary bladder stores urine until elimination.
questions.trap: Ureters transport urine; urethra exits it.

questions.prompt: Myelin is best described as:
questions.why: Myelin insulates axons to speed nerve impulse transmission.
questions.trap: Myelin is not a hormone or enzyme.

questions.prompt: Which hormone is commonly associated with the fight-or-flight response?
questions.why: Epinephrine supports acute stress responses such as increased heart rate and alertness.
questions.trap: Thyroxine regulates metabolism but isn’t the primary fight-or-flight hormone.

questions.prompt: Which muscle is the main driver of quiet inhalation?
questions.why: The diaphragm contracts to increase thoracic volume for inhalation.
questions.trap: Internal intercostals are mainly involved in forced exhalation.

questions.prompt: Which of the following organ produces bile?
questions.why: The liver produces bile; the gallbladder stores it.
questions.trap: The gallbladder stores bile but does not produce it.

questions.prompt: Which organelle is most associated with lipid synthesis and detoxification?
questions.why: Smooth ER synthesizes lipids and participates in detox processes.
questions.trap: Rough ER is more associated with protein synthesis via ribosomes.

questions.prompt: A motor unit is best described as:

choices.D: A motor neuron and the muscle fibers it innervates

questions.answer: A motor neuron and the muscle fibers it innervates
questions.why: Motor units determine how muscle fibers are activated for movement.
questions.trap: Sarcomeres are contractile units inside fibers.

questions.prompt: The kidneys help maintain homeostasis by:

choices.B: Regulating fluid, electrolytes, and waste removal

questions.answer: Regulating fluid, electrolytes, and waste removal
questions.why: Kidneys regulate water, electrolytes, and excrete metabolic wastes.
questions.trap: Digestive enzymes are produced by pancreas, not kidneys.

questions.prompt: Cortisol is primarily produced by the:
questions.why: The adrenal cortex produces cortisol, involved in stress and metabolism regulation.
questions.trap: The adrenal medulla produces epinephrine/norepinephrine.

questions.prompt: Which gland produces insulin?
questions.why: Insulin is produced by beta cells in the pancreas to regulate blood glucose.
questions.trap: Adrenal cortex produces cortisol, not insulin.

questions.prompt: Which of the following vessel carries oxygenated blood from the lungs back to the heart?
questions.why: Pulmonary veins return oxygenated blood from lungs to the left atrium.
questions.trap: Pulmonary arteries carry deoxygenated blood to the lungs.

questions.prompt: What is the main functional unit of the kidney?
questions.why: Nephrons filter blood and form urine via filtration, reabsorption, and secretion.
questions.trap: Neurons are nerve cells, not kidney units.

questions.prompt: Sebaceous glands primarily secrete:
questions.why: Sebaceous glands secrete oil that lubricates skin and hair.
questions.trap: Sweat glands secrete sweat; bile is produced by liver.

questions.prompt: The primary nitrogenous waste product in urine is:
questions.why: Urea is produced from amino acid metabolism and excreted by kidneys.
questions.trap: Glucose is normally reabsorbed and is not a primary waste.

questions.prompt: Which organ produces most digestive enzyme secretion for the small intestine?
questions.why: The pancreas produces enzymes that digest fats, proteins, and carbohydrates.
questions.trap: The gallbladder stores bile; it doesn't produce digestive enzymes.

questions.prompt: Which protein binds calcium to allow contraction to proceed?
questions.why: Calcium binds troponin, shifting tropomyosin to expose binding sites on actin.
questions.trap: Tropomyosin blocks sites until troponin shifts it.

questions.prompt: Which of the following organ is primarily responsible for detoxifying blood and metabolizing many substances?
questions.why: The liver metabolizes many substances and supports detoxification and bile production.
questions.trap: Kidneys filter blood for urine production; they are not the primary detox metabolism organ.

questions.prompt: Myoglobin’s primary role is to:
questions.why: Myoglobin stores oxygen for use during muscle activity.
questions.trap: Clotting is platelets; nerve impulses are neurons.

questions.prompt: Which hormone increases blood calcium levels?
questions.why: PTH raises blood calcium via effects on bone and kidneys.
questions.trap: ADH regulates water balance, not calcium.

questions.prompt: Aldosterone primarily helps regulate:
questions.why: Aldosterone increases sodium reabsorption, influencing water retention and blood pressure.
questions.trap: Oxygen regulation is respiratory/circulatory, not aldosterone’s primary role.

questions.prompt: Which blood vessels carry blood back to the heart?
questions.why: Veins return blood to the heart, often aided by valves.
questions.trap: Arteries carry blood away from the heart.

questions.prompt: Which structure is the basic contractile unit of skeletal muscle?
questions.why: Sarcomeres contain actin and myosin that slide to produce contraction.
questions.trap: Nephrons filter; alveoli exchange gases; osteons are bone units.

questions.prompt: Synapses are best described as:

choices.C: Junctions where neurons communicate via neurotransmitters

questions.answer: Junctions where neurons communicate via neurotransmitters
questions.why: Synapses allow signal transfer between neurons.
questions.trap: Sarcomeres contract; nephrons filter; alveoli exchange gas.

questions.prompt: Carbon dioxide is primarily carried in the blood as:
questions.why: Most CO2 is transported as bicarbonate in plasma.
questions.trap: Oxygen transport differs and is mainly hemoglobin-bound.

questions.prompt: What is the main function of lymph nodes?

choices.C: Filter lymph and support immune responses

questions.answer: Filter lymph and support immune responses
questions.why: Lymph nodes filter lymph and house immune cells for immune response.
questions.trap: Bile is produced by the liver.

questions.prompt: The central nervous system includes the:
questions.why: CNS consists of the brain and spinal cord.
questions.trap: Cranial and spinal nerves are part of the peripheral nervous system.

questions.prompt: What is the primary function of mitochondria?
questions.why: Mitochondria generate ATP, providing energy for cellular processes.
questions.trap: Ribosomes are tempting because they’re essential, but they synthesize proteins—not ATP.

questions.prompt: Pulse pressure is the difference between:
questions.why: Pulse pressure is calculated as systolic minus diastolic pressure.
questions.trap: Venous vs arterial is not pulse pressure.

questions.prompt: Glucagon primarily acts to:
questions.why: Glucagon raises blood glucose by promoting glycogen breakdown and glucose release.
questions.trap: Insulin lowers glucose; glucagon raises it.

questions.prompt: Which organ stores bile?
questions.why: The gallbladder stores and concentrates bile produced by the liver.
questions.trap: The liver makes bile; it doesn't primarily store it.

questions.prompt: A normal heart rhythm is initiated by which structure?
questions.why: The SA node is the primary pacemaker of the heart.
questions.trap: The AV node relays signals but is not the primary pacemaker.

questions.prompt: What is a primary function of the lymphatic system?

choices.C: Return interstitial fluid to the bloodstream and support immune function

questions.answer: Return interstitial fluid to the bloodstream and support immune function
questions.why: The lymphatic system returns fluid to circulation and supports immune surveillance.
questions.trap: Pumping blood is a cardiovascular function.

questions.prompt: What is the primary function of hydrochloric acid in the stomach?

choices.B: Helps break down proteins and activates enzymes

questions.answer: Helps break down proteins and activates enzymes
questions.why: Stomach acid denatures proteins and supports activation of digestive enzymes.
questions.trap: Absorption is mainly in the small intestine.

questions.prompt: Where is calcium stored in a skeletal muscle cell prior to contraction?
questions.why: The sarcoplasmic reticulum stores calcium and releases it to enable contraction.
questions.trap: Mitochondria produce ATP, but they are not the primary calcium store for contraction.

questions.prompt: Glomerular filtration primarily occurs in the:
questions.why: Filtration starts at the glomerulus where blood is filtered into Bowman’s capsule.
questions.trap: Alveoli are for gas exchange, not filtration.

questions.prompt: Where does most nutrient absorption occur?
questions.why: Most nutrient absorption occurs in the small intestine due to villi and large surface area.
questions.trap: The large intestine primarily absorbs water.

questions.prompt: Which organ produces many digestive enzymes?
questions.why: The pancreas secretes enzymes into the small intestine for digestion.
questions.trap: The gallbladder stores bile; it does not secrete digestive enzymes.

questions.prompt: Which of the following cells are primarily responsible for producing antibodies?
questions.why: B cells differentiate into plasma cells that produce antibodies.
questions.trap: Red blood cells carry oxygen; they do not produce antibodies.

questions.prompt: Which part of the neuron receives incoming signals from other neurons?
questions.why: Dendrites receive synaptic input; the axon carries output signals.
questions.trap: Myelin insulates; it doesn't receive signals.

questions.prompt: Which organelle is primarily responsible for protein synthesis?
questions.why: Ribosomes translate mRNA into proteins.
questions.trap: Golgi packages proteins but does not synthesize them.

questions.prompt: Which of the following structure contains the vocal folds?
questions.why: The larynx houses vocal folds used for phonation.
questions.trap: Bronchi are lower airways, not the voice box.

questions.prompt: Which neurotransmitter is commonly involved at the neuromuscular junction?
questions.why: Acetylcholine triggers depolarization at the motor end plate.
questions.trap: Epinephrine is a stress hormone, not the main NMJ transmitter.

questions.prompt: Peristalsis refers to:

choices.A: Wave-like muscular contractions that move food

questions.answer: Wave-like muscular contractions that move food
questions.why: Peristalsis propels food along the digestive tract via smooth muscle contractions.
questions.trap: Gas exchange occurs at alveoli, not via peristalsis.

questions.prompt: Which of the following structure connects the brain to the spinal cord and controls basic life functions?
questions.why: The brainstem regulates breathing, heart rate, and other vital functions.
questions.trap: The cerebellum coordinates movement but does not control primary life support.

questions.prompt: Which type of vessel is the primary site of exchange of gases and nutrients?
questions.why: Capillaries have thin walls for diffusion of gases and nutrients.
questions.trap: Arterioles regulate flow but are not primary exchange sites.

questions.prompt: Rough endoplasmic reticulum is most directly involved in:

choices.D: Protein processing for secretion or membranes

questions.answer: Protein processing for secretion or membranes
questions.why: Rough ER (with ribosomes) supports synthesis/processing of proteins destined for membranes or secretion.
questions.trap: Gas exchange occurs in alveoli, not rough ER.

questions.prompt: Which of the following structure is the primary site of oxygen binding in blood?
questions.why: Hemoglobin in red blood cells binds oxygen for transport.
questions.trap: Albumin transports substances but is not the main oxygen-binding molecule.

questions.prompt: Which ion directly triggers actin-myosin interaction?
questions.why: Calcium exposure of binding sites enables cross-bridge formation.
questions.trap: Sodium/potassium create action potentials but do not expose binding sites.

questions.prompt: Which blood component is most responsible for fighting infection?
questions.why: White blood cells support immune defense against pathogens.
questions.trap: Platelets help clot; RBCs transport oxygen.

questions.prompt: DNA is primarily located in which organelle in human cells?
questions.why: In eukaryotic cells, most DNA is housed in the nucleus.
questions.trap: Mitochondria contain some DNA, but most is nuclear.

questions.prompt: Melatonin is primarily associated with:
questions.why: Melatonin from the pineal gland helps regulate circadian rhythms.
questions.trap: Oxygen transport is via hemoglobin, not melatonin.

questions.prompt: What is the main function of the epiglottis?

choices.C: Prevents food from entering the airway during swallowing

questions.answer: Prevents food from entering the airway during swallowing
questions.why: The epiglottis covers the airway during swallowing to prevent aspiration.
questions.trap: Vocal sound production occurs at the vocal folds, not the epiglottis.

questions.prompt: Tidal volume is best defined as:

choices.B: The amount of air moved in a normal quiet breath
choices.C: Maximum air exhaled after maximal inhalation

questions.answer: The amount of air moved in a normal quiet breath
questions.why: Tidal volume is inhaled/exhaled volume during quiet breathing.
questions.trap: Residual volume remains after maximal exhalation.

questions.prompt: Which structure regulates what enters and exits the cell?
questions.why: The cell membrane is selectively permeable and controls transport into and out of the cell.
questions.trap: The nucleus stores DNA but does not regulate cell-boundary transport.

questions.prompt: Which structure produces sweat?
questions.why: Sweat glands secrete sweat for thermoregulation.
questions.trap: Sebaceous glands produce oil (sebum), not sweat.

questions.prompt: Which of the following structure is responsible for creating most cellular proteins?
questions.why: Ribosomes are the sites where proteins are assembled.
questions.trap: The nucleolus helps assemble ribosomal components, but ribosomes synthesize proteins.

questions.prompt: The primary function of lysosomes is to:

choices.A: Break down waste and cellular debris

questions.answer: Break down waste and cellular debris
questions.why: Lysosomes contain enzymes that digest waste and damaged organelles.
questions.trap: ATP production occurs in mitochondria, not lysosomes.

questions.prompt: Urine exits the body through the:
questions.why: The urethra carries urine from bladder to outside the body.
questions.trap: Ureters connect kidneys to bladder.

questions.prompt: Which of the following event occurs during ventricular systole?

choices.B: Blood is ejected from the ventricles

questions.answer: Blood is ejected from the ventricles
questions.why: Systole is contraction; ventricular contraction ejects blood into arteries.
questions.trap: Ventricular filling occurs in diastole.

questions.prompt: What is the primary function of myelin?

choices.B: Increases conduction speed along the nerve fiber

questions.answer: Increases conduction speed along the nerve fiber
questions.why: Myelin insulates axons and increases signal conduction speed.
questions.trap: Neurotransmitters are produced by neurons, not myelin.

questions.prompt: The hypothalamus is most directly involved in:

choices.C: Homeostasis and endocrine regulation via the pituitary

questions.answer: Homeostasis and endocrine regulation via the pituitary
questions.why: The hypothalamus helps regulate temperature, hunger, and pituitary hormone control.
questions.trap: Urine formation is kidney; gas exchange is lung.

questions.prompt: Which of the following structure carries deoxygenated blood from the body back to the right atrium?
questions.why: The vena cavae return systemic blood to the right atrium.
questions.trap: The aorta carries blood away from the heart, not to it.

questions.prompt: Which cell type produces keratin in the epidermis?
questions.why: Keratinocytes are epidermal cells that produce keratin.
questions.trap: Melanocytes produce melanin, not keratin.

questions.prompt: ATP is required in muscle contraction primarily to:

choices.A: Detach myosin from actin and power cross-bridge cycling

questions.answer: Detach myosin from actin and power cross-bridge cycling
questions.why: ATP enables myosin detachment and provides energy for cross-bridge cycling.
questions.trap: Oxygen storage is via myoglobin, not ATP.

questions.prompt: Which of the following structure carries oxygenated blood from the left ventricle to the body?
questions.why: The aorta distributes oxygenated blood from the left ventricle to the systemic circulation.
questions.trap: Pulmonary artery carries deoxygenated blood to lungs, not systemic outflow.

questions.prompt: Which hormone lowers blood glucose by promoting cellular uptake?
questions.why: Insulin lowers blood glucose by facilitating uptake and storage.
questions.trap: Glucagon raises blood glucose; cortisol supports stress metabolism.

questions.prompt: Which process moves oxygen from alveoli into pulmonary capillaries?
questions.why: Gases move down partial pressure gradients via diffusion across membranes.
questions.trap: Osmosis is water movement, not gas movement.

questions.prompt: Which of the following component of blood is primarily responsible for clotting?
questions.why: Platelets aggregate to form plugs and support clot formation.
questions.trap: White blood cells fight infection; they do not form clots.

questions.prompt: Which part of the nervous system controls voluntary skeletal muscle movement?
questions.why: The somatic nervous system controls voluntary movement of skeletal muscles.
questions.trap: Autonomic divisions control involuntary functions.

questions.prompt: Which organelle is primarily responsible for breaking down cellular waste using enzymes?
questions.why: Lysosomes contain digestive enzymes that break down debris and waste.
questions.trap: Mitochondria make ATP; ribosomes make proteins.

questions.prompt: Which of the following heart chamber primarily pumps oxygenated blood into the systemic circulation?
questions.why: The left ventricle generates the force needed to send oxygenated blood through systemic circulation.
questions.trap: Aorta is a vessel, not a chamber.

questions.prompt: Reabsorption in the nephron refers to:

choices.B: Returning useful substances from filtrate to blood

questions.answer: Returning useful substances from filtrate to blood
questions.why: Reabsorption moves substances like glucose and ions back into the bloodstream.
questions.trap: Filtration is the first step; reabsorption is the return step.

questions.prompt: Which layer of skin is the outermost protective layer?
questions.why: The epidermis forms the outer barrier surface of skin.
questions.trap: Dermis is deeper and contains vessels and nerves.

questions.prompt: Which of the following structure is primarily responsible for producing sound for speech?
questions.why: Vocal folds vibrate to produce sound; the larynx houses them.
questions.trap: Epiglottis protects airway during swallowing; it doesn't create sound.

questions.prompt: Which glial cell forms myelin in the peripheral nervous system?
questions.why: Schwann cells myelinate peripheral nerves.
questions.trap: Oligodendrocytes myelinate CNS axons.

questions.prompt: Which muscle is most associated with forced exhalation?
questions.why: Forced exhalation uses abdominal muscles to increase pressure and expel air.
questions.trap: Quiet exhalation is usually passive.

questions.prompt: Which of the following organelle modifies and packages proteins for secretion?
questions.why: The Golgi processes and packages proteins and lipids for transport.
questions.trap: Ribosomes make proteins but don’t package them for secretion.

questions.prompt: Which term describes the movement of air into and out of the lungs?
questions.why: Ventilation is airflow; diffusion is gas exchange across membranes.
questions.trap: Perfusion refers to blood flow, not airflow.

questions.prompt: Which organ is commonly associated with filtering blood and immune surveillance?
questions.why: The spleen filters blood and supports immune functions.
questions.trap: Gallbladder stores bile; it does not filter blood.

questions.prompt: Which of the following division of the autonomic nervous system is most associated with rest-and-digest?
questions.why: Parasympathetic activity supports digestion and recovery.
questions.trap: Sympathetic supports fight-or-flight.

questions.prompt: What causes a muscle fiber to relax after contraction?

choices.B: Calcium is pumped back into the sarcoplasmic reticulum

questions.answer: Calcium is pumped back into the sarcoplasmic reticulum
questions.why: Relaxation occurs as calcium is removed from the cytosol and cross-bridges stop cycling.
questions.trap: ATP is used but not 'destroyed' as the relaxation mechanism.

questions.prompt: Which process describes movement of water across a membrane from low solute to high solute?
questions.why: Osmosis is water movement across a semipermeable membrane down a concentration gradient.
questions.trap: Diffusion is solute/gas movement; ventilation is airflow.

questions.prompt: The hypodermis is mainly composed of:
questions.why: The hypodermis contains fat and connective tissue for insulation and cushioning.
questions.trap: Cartilage and bone are not primary hypodermis components.

questions.prompt: Which process describes cell division for growth and repair?
questions.why: Mitosis produces genetically identical cells for growth and repair.
questions.trap: Meiosis produces gametes, not typical body-cell growth.
